place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: On 24 April 2025, at approximately 4:30 PM, a group of students in school uniforms were observed loitering and sitting on the floor at the entrance of the Cheers outlet located in Marine Terrace MRT station. While the students appeared to be taking a break or socializing, their presence created minor obstructions near the convenience store and along the public walkway. Such behavior may disrupt the flow of commuters and create an untidy impression of the station. It is recommended that the respective schools be informed of this incident, so appropriate reminders on public conduct and responsibility in uniform can be issued. Students should be encouraged to gather in more appropriate common areas or community spaces, and avoid sitting in commercial or high-traffic zones, especially in public transport facilities. Ensuring that students are guided with respect and accountability will maintain both their safety and the comfort of the broader public. MRT staff or school liaison officers may wish to follow up if such behavior persists during peak hours or in similar public settings.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Newly open TEL line that brings residents in the east and along the North-South Corridor closer to a train station and hence giving them faster access to the city.
Marine Parade MRT station is an underground Mass Rapid Transit station on the Thomson–East Coast line (TEL). The station is built underneath Marine Parade Road, serving nearby residential and commercial developments including Parkway Parade, Roxy Square and I12 Katong. Exit A next to McDonalds and Marine Parade Food Centre where you can find varieties of local delights.
Worth noting this station house Singapore's first underground bicycle parking facilities.
Direct link to Parkway Parade shopping mall have yet to be connected, once done it will be seamless connection the shopping mall.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: New mrt station in the neighborhood. Well designed and build, comes with underground bicycle park and open air car park nearby. It’s so quiet that I didn’t realize a train had arrived! Able to reach Outram Part in 20min (instead of 23min using the Green Line). Environment was clean and very spacious!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: This mrt station is super long in length, it takes a good 5 mins to walk from one end to the other. other than that, it's a standard mrt station with a cheers and bike park, nothing too interesting unlike marine parade which looks like the Xbox logo, but I'll give it 5 stars anyways since it does it's job well. It serves a lot of school students so yeah good job

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Thomson East Coast Line (TEL) A very much needed addition to the est side , connecting easties to city and orchard .

Totally dig the huge ventilation fans for cool air circulation .

You can take the train to Marina Bay and switch to red line towards Orchard or continue on the TEL  to Orchard .

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Nice and quiet.  Library staff are helpful. But mobile data signal by Simba ( 1 bar inside, 2 -3 bars outside. not sure about the others) is really poor. Couldn't even login to "checking and secure my seat booking", thus the booking got canceled,  and went to someone else.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Queenstown library is a heritage building, rich in its own history. Spacious with two levels, kids area is on the first floor. The second floor for adults is really quiet with nice view over a green park.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
building's exterior may look unassuming or even a lil spooky but the inside is really cozy and homely! lovely space, limited tables but plenty of seats

if you really want a table, they have a table booking system you can use to ensure you have one when you get there!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Nostalgia a plenty in this well preserved library that houses many childhood memories for some.

Located in the quiet side of Queenstown facing sky residences @ Dawson, short walk from Queenstown MRT, a cozy library to spend your time here.

2 levels served by a lift & stairs, lots of reading areas and I particularly enjoy the level 2 quiet reading room facing the greenery.

There are lots of nostalgia and nice touches to this library.

Take your time to explore, read, borrow or exchange. what a privilege!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
As of April 2026: It is a old school library with the kinda of nostalgia you get when you visited a library in the 2000's.

Most of the Library's floor space is dedicated to books with working space being rather limited. It's not very convenient to access either as you gotta walk abit from the MRT station and not much amenities nearby.

The tables tend to get booked out until like 7pm ish. With patrons playing musical chairs where someone who has a booking asks those who didn't make a booking to move.

Interesting mix of patrons, with elderly men being the majority and somehow quite a few expats.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This place has been around since we were young. And it is known to host many shops that sells the products for military, police and such.

Lets dive in!

Food:
There have been changes with some maintaining their business around. Most of the time, many would come for tulang or mamak dishes. We kinda like the western food here that is around recently.

Service:
Many shop owners will call you for their business, part of their attention seeking modus operandi. But dont worry bout them, as all seats in this area can be for anyone, thus no dedicated table for any booth.

Atmosphere:
Having been around here for years, we do feel the charm and miss the memories we had while younger here. There have been some works on the place and its way cleaner now compared to the past.

All in all, we definitely enjoy coming over, even for window shopping at the top floors. We applogise there are limited photos of the shops around as we were too hungry actually 🤭🤭

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Besides the ever-popular claypot rice & Hokkien prawn mee (which had a long queue despite the odd hours), try out Dragon Curry’s Nan ru pork cutlet curry rice! Sweet curry with a lemongrass taste, thin pork cutlet but quite tender.

Wen Li Taiwanese’s Pig intestine mee sua set was tasty too, & comes with crispy salt & pepper chicken! For dessert, go for 75 Ah Balling’s Tang yuan with peanut soup / almond milk.

IG @pingguo301

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There are actually three levels at Golden Mile Food Centre. Most will visit the 1st and 2nd level where many good and popular food stalls are located but little people visit the 3rd level where shops selling military merchandise are located. Do pay a visit while you’re there to see the many interesting merchandises on display.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Not able to fairly gage the food here because it has lots of food stalls I'd only tried the glutinous ball peanut soup that tasted just like my mom's home made peanut soup yum2. Generous portion of peanuts! And 6 glutinous balls or tang yuan made of 5 different flavors you can choose from at SGD3.50 wow! I ate 2 bowls and I came back again for another 2 bowls! I brought company hahaha~ 😋😋

A Sarawak friend i brought thinks the auntie made the 5 flavor glutinous balls herself because the thickness of the tangyuan were not even or same with other dessert stalls.

Personally I still love peanut and sesame flavors best. But I'm pleasantly surprised by green tea or matcha flavor. My friends love the yam flavor. I like red bean paste too but peanut and sesame are sweeter and crunchier with something to bite I needed more of those 2 flavors or I'll get a 3rd bowl. 🤣🤣

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This Halal stall next to Ashes Burnnit, fried delicious Kway Teow Goreng. The noodle is abit moist if anyone like it fried this way. The prawn is cut in smaller size & fresh too. Portion is good for heavy lunch at S$4.50, however is a little chilly, so prepare a cold drink in hand.
I had try their Laska Johor before which is creamy & different from SG laksa.
Stall mainly served seafood dishes, No beef & mutton.

Jan’26, revisited the stall and managed to photo the stall name. Ordered the Mee Goreng also Kway Teow Goreng and she take time to fried it. The taste remain same as last eaten but this time less spicy. The Mee Goreng is slightly on a wet/moist dish that differs from traditional Indian mee goreng with potato.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
East Coast area and wanted to share my thoughts on East Coast Park. It remains one of the best-maintained spaces in Singapore, and its extensive connectivity to other parks makes it an ideal destination for long walks or cycling trips. I particularly enjoyed the ability to ride my bicycle all the way to Gardens by the Bay via the park connector; it was always such a calm and refreshing experience.

The park offers a wonderful atmosphere with its blend of lush greenery and sea views. It is exceptionally well-equipped for families and fitness enthusiasts, featuring dedicated exercise stations, children’s play areas, and wide, separate paths for cycling and walking that prevent the space from feeling crowded.

Beyond the scenery, the facilities are excellent. There are convenient bicycle rentals, a variety of dining options in the restaurant area where you can enjoy the sea breeze, and well-maintained BBQ pits and shelters. For those interested in staying longer, the designated camping areas are a great touch, and I’ve always found the washrooms and bathing facilities to be very clean.

Overall, it is a perfect place to relax, exercise, and enjoy nature, whether you are visiting alone or with friends and family.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
East Coast Park is one of the best and most well-maintained parks in Singapore. It is a very big park and is beautifully connected to other parks across Singapore, making it perfect for long walks or cycling trips. The park has lots of nice scenery, with greenery and sea views that make the atmosphere very relaxing. In some areas, there are exercise equipment stations and children’s play areas, which are great for families and fitness lovers.

There is a dedicated cycling path and a separate walking path, both of which are wide and well maintained, so it never feels crowded. You can also rent bicycles, and there are fun activities and attractions that bring in many people. The park has a restaurant area where you can enjoy a variety of foods while feeling the cool sea breeze. You can book BBQ pits, and there are huts available for shelter in case of rain. There are also designated camping areas for those who want to stay overnight.

I used to live near the East Coast area, and what I loved most was riding my bicycle all the way to Gardens by the Bay, as the park connector makes this possible. It was always a calm and refreshing experience. On top of that, the washrooms and bathing areas are very clean and well maintained. Overall, East Coast Park is a perfect place to relax, exercise, eat, and enjoy nature, whether alone, with friends, or with family.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Nice place to walk, enjoy the sea breeze. Or one can enjoy coffee/drinks at one of the restaurant or cafe or even hawker centre. Cycling or inline blading can let u exercise and head to less crowded spot on the further end of the beach.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This is one of the most beautiful parks I’ve ever visited in Singapore.

It has plenty of trees, clean walking paths, and comfortable benches where people can relax and enjoy the peaceful atmosphere. I especially recommend visiting in the morning because the weather is cooler and the park feels very refreshing

There are also many trash bins around the park, so it is convenient for visitors who bring drinks or snacks. The area is well maintained, clean, and perfect for taking a walk, reading a book, or simply spending quiet time away from the busy city. 🌳

Beach view at the side and can swim. Bedok jetty , fishing.

review_text: Thank you Veronica for helping me to settle the Singpass matters. Also Karen too for your help re the SG seniors vouchers. These are 2 beautiful, kind n patient souls!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Has a sheltered basketball court that is used for everything except basketball. Ring is missing on one side of the court.

Had so much potential as one of the only few sheltered basketball courts in the area but unfortunately poorly maintained and not used to its potential.

Can you imagine a basketball court that is booked every evening (except Tuesdays) for non-basketball related activities? This is it.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: This is a large swimming complex and great for kids on the 5th floor. There are 3 pools. One for small kiddies, one intermediate and one advanced. Pool is not heated but good for warm weather days. The Olympic pool has a shelter. There is an activesg gym on the 6th floor that overlooks the pool if you want to work out (separate entrance fee of 2.50 sgd). However, the gym are strict with footwear and need to bring your own towel.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Parkway Parade has that rare old school Singapore mall charm that newer malls still cannot really replicate. It is not the fanciest mall around anymore, but that is honestly part of the appeal. The place feels comfortable, familiar, and genuinely lived in by East side families instead of trying too hard to become a luxury lifestyle destination.

You can pretty much do everything here in one trip. Good food options, decent retail variety, supermarkets, electronics, enrichment centres, cafes, and random nostalgic shops that somehow still survive after all these years. The recent refreshes and additions like MUJI and the revamped food options helped modernise the mall without completely removing its identity. Direct MRT connectivity also makes visiting way more convenient now.  ￼

What I still enjoy most is the atmosphere. Compared to some mega malls that feel stressful and overcrowded, Parkway Parade feels more relaxed and easy to navigate. There is a strong sense of nostalgia here especially for people who grew up in the East. Even online discussions always mention memories of the old arcades, bookstores, rooftop playgrounds, and family weekends spent here.  ￼

Some parts of the mall do feel slightly dated and there are noticeably more vacant units recently, but overall it still remains one of the most iconic and dependable suburban malls in Singapore. Perfect place for a chill weekend without the chaos of town.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: One of the best national libraries I’ve seen, with a large number of books to rent. 24/7 book drop, a lot of magazines available to read, plenty of quiet, comfy and beautiful areas to read in, and that’s all just the basement level – there are 15 floors all with their own content. Floors 5, 10 and 15 have green areas if you’d like to be in a forest-like environment while reading/working/studying. If you’re nearby, surely a great place to visit!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Perhaps the largest public library in Singapore. Hard to find a librarian for assistance, and bookings for seats are required if you do not want to be asked to leave just because someone has booked the seats. A really stupid concept for those who would casually come into the library if they are in the area. Throughout the years, NLB has done nothing to enhance the library experience. What the public has been slapped with are rules and regulations which makes visiting the library a chore.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Came purely for the kids section of the library and it sure lived up to our expectations. The little one thoroughly enjoyed himself amidst the aquarium themed library and the endless literature around the area. Very thoughtfully, they also offer floor cushions where you can take and just find a spot to read and chill out on. The hit area with the deck chairs is the central of the kids section but was unfortunately pretty much always filled with people. Great place to spend the weekend letting the kids immerse themselves. Parking is also pretty reasonable at $1.20 per hour on Sundays.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: The Central Public Library in Singapore is a vibrant space blending innovation, design, and functionality. Located at the National Library Building, it offers an extensive collection of books, digital resources, and themed spaces catering to all age groups. Highlights include the eco-friendly My Tree House, the world’s first green library for children, and its lush ambiance. The library’s modern facilities, cozy reading nooks, and seamless technology make it an ideal place for study or leisure. Regular workshops and events add to its community spirit. A hub of knowledge and creativity, it’s a must-visit for book lovers and lifelong learners.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Today, I attended Emma Memma's Performance at Drama Centre. I grew up watching Sesame Street and Hi-5, and of course, The Wiggles. So when I learned that Emma Memma, formerly of The Wiggles, was performing, I was really excited.

What I find especially meaningful is the way Emma Memma and Elvin Melvin connects through sign language. For that, kudos to them.

Some children can only sing and communicate through sign language — yet they’re often overlooked in theatre shows. That’s why Emma Memma’s performances stand out: she reminds us that inclusion matters, and every child deserves to shine. (Catch her in action on youtube.)

Drop by our libraries or check out the NLB online portal and mobile app! You’ll find a huge variety of e-books, e-magazines, audiobooks, and lots of other exciting features. Truly one of the best resources you can enjoy in Singapore 🙂

Have you seen the sky-bridged library, on the upper floors, rising 16 storeys high? Designed with floor-to-ceiling glass panels, it welcomes natural light into every corner, creating a warm, open atmosphere. Look out for the greenery that softens the sleek architecture, blending nature with structure to offer a calming, inviting space.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A crowded megamall in Singapore. Can go there directly by MRT,  very convenient! The malls have so many shoplots, restaurants and cafes. A lot of branded shops can be found at here.  You can go Sentosa Singapore from here too. There is a park at rooftop as well, very chilling and relaxing. Now they have new year decoration right outside of the entrance, very attractive!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Really enjoyed visiting VivoCity 🛍️
The mall is HUGE and has pretty much everything you need. Lots of shopping, food options, cafes, and entertainment all in one place. It’s also super convenient since it connects directly to Sentosa, which makes it a great stop before or after exploring the island 🌴🚆
One of my favorite parts was definitely the terrace area 😍 The outdoor space was so pretty and relaxing, especially with the open view, greenery, and waterfront atmosphere. It felt really nice to just sit there for a while and enjoy the vibe, especially around sunset 🌅✨ Such a nice contrast from the busy indoor mall area.
The mall itself was clean, spacious, and easy to walk around despite how big it is. I also liked that there were plenty of seating areas to rest between shopping and exploring. Overall, VivoCity felt more than just a shopping mall, it was honestly a really enjoyable place to spend a few hours 🤍

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This mega mall that serves the residents in the west side of Singapore,  has upgraded recently with the introduction of more eateries at the basement, when one exits from the mrt station. Singapore is a foodie haven , so it is important to pump in popular brands to draw the hungry working people n tourists.  One can see that every outlet is packed during meal time. So it is very competitive for the shops to come up with promotional set menu to attract customers.  Another local pastime is shopping, so sale events are usually held at the front podium area. The mall houses two floors for those who enjoy shopping.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
So many stores to choose from, this mall is huge!
The Christmas Market was great, it was lit nicely and there were a lot of good Christmas shopping options like baked goods, wrapping paper, Christmas signs and cards.
Everything was very clean but since its a huge mall it does get very very busy.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Visited the National Library Building mainly for The Albatross File: Singapore’s Independence Declassified exhibition, and it turned out to be a meaningful experience. The exhibition itself is relatively small, but the historical significance is immense. It provides a fascinating and candid look into the events leading up to Singapore’s separation from Malaysia in 1965, including documents and notes showing that both sides had, by then, come to see separation as the likely path forward. Seeing the actual letters, transcripts, and handwritten notes made this pivotal chapter of Singapore’s history feel very real and personal

I also took some time to roam around the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library and was impressed by how incredibly well managed the entire place is. The building itself is architecturally striking, modern, spacious, and filled with natural light. The library environment feels calm, organized, and conducive for learning and research, with clear signage and well-maintained facilities throughout

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
2026 January - Did quick look around, enjoyed reading the stories from the mini exhibition Knots of Time. Looks like there are other exhibitions and all sorts of collection, will be back to explore and read.

Great ambience, nice for researchers and the public alike. Place has plenty of space if you'd like to get some work done, offers brilliant views, and even has a skygarden. Power and water is readily available, and theres even sufficient space for a stroll if you're feeling a mental block coming on.

Will be back to check out the albatross files. The atrium at the bottom is very pleasant, and the vaguely 'heritage' old chang kee seems like a good place to check out as well.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Albatross exhibition of Singapore

Thankful for this curated history that bares all that Singaporeans may not know. We had the privilege of having seniors in their 70s who came along, cementing our understanding of what they had gone thru.

The whole setup allowed us to go at our own pace and the voice of our late and retired ministers add a sense of urgency and realism.

A suggestion for those who are not English educated, as the ministers are speaking in English, please allow translator set made avail in various language to our non English speaking seniors.

Lastly the wooden board is super heavy. Made it very difficult for us to lift up the board to read the files.  Lastly is there a need to have the board hidden? Most don’t know it exist until someone lifted it up.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Visited the National Library Building mainly for The Albatross File: Singapore’s Independence Declassified exhibition, and it turned out to be a meaningful experience. The exhibition itself is relatively small, but the historical significance is immense. It provides a fascinating and candid look into the events leading up to Singapore’s separation from Malaysia in 1965, including documents and notes showing that both sides had, by then, come to see separation as the likely path forward. Seeing the actual letters, transcripts, and handwritten notes made this pivotal chapter of Singapore’s history feel very real and personal

I also took some time to roam around the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library and was impressed by how incredibly well managed the entire place is. The building itself is architecturally striking, modern, spacious, and filled with natural light. The library environment feels calm, organized, and conducive for learning and research, with clear signage and well-maintained facilities throughout

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Gardens by the Bay is truly an amazing place and definitely a must-visit attraction in Singapore. The entire area is beautifully designed, extremely clean, and very well maintained, making it a wonderful experience for visitors of all ages.

The Flower Dome and Cloud Forest offer a unique opportunity to see plants and flowers from different parts of the world. During our visit, we also experienced the Tulip Feast, where we saw many beautiful varieties of tulips in full bloom. The colorful display was absolutely stunning and added a vibrant touch to the overall experience. The indoor waterfall inside the Cloud Forest is especially impressive and creates a refreshing atmosphere. The Supertree Grove is another major highlight, and the night light show there feels absolutely magical with the music and colorful lights.

The whole place is spacious and easy to explore, although there is a lot to walk around, so it can feel a little tiring after some time. Still, the beautiful views and attractions make the walking completely worth it.

There are many perfect photo spots throughout the gardens, especially during the evening when the night view looks stunning. Souvenir shops are also available if you would like to buy gifts or memories from your visit.

Overall, it’s a peaceful and beautiful place to relax, enjoy nature, and experience one of Singapore’s most iconic attractions. Highly recommended, especially for first-time visitors.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Gardens by the Bay is truly a highlight of Singapore and absolutely not to be missed. I’d highly recommend getting a combo ticket for the Cloud Forest and Flower Dome—plan for about 2-3 hours to cover both.

The Cloud Forest is spectacular, almost Jurassic Park-like in parts, and enjoyable for both kids and adults. There’s misting at specific times, so it’s worth checking those in advance. A great way to explore is to head to the top level first and then make your way down, stopping at the different exhibits along the way.
The Flower Dome is equally beautiful, showcasing flowers and plants from around the world, with plenty of photo-worthy corners throughout. There’s also a nice gift shop with garden and flower-themed souvenirs.

I also got a separate ticket for the OCBC Skyway and it was 100% worth it. I went up right at 7:45 PM during the light and sound show and got to watch it from the top—an incredible experience. You can stay up for about 15 minutes, and while there’s an elevator, lines to go can get long.

For food, the Jurassic-themed food court nearby is a good option for dinner. Overall, it felt like a well-spent ~$60 to explore all three places. I’d suggest going after 3 PM and spending the entire evening there to fully enjoy the whole area.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Gardens by the Bay is absolutely magical and one of my favorite spots in Singapore. The Supertree Grove is stunning, especially at night when the light and sound show begins—it feels like stepping into a fairy tale. I loved walking through the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest; both are beautifully maintained and full of unique plants and flowers from around the world. The pathways and gardens are perfect for leisurely strolls, and there are so many spots to take photos or just pause and enjoy the scenery. It’s peaceful, inspiring, and feels like a green oasis right in the middle of the city. Whether you’re a nature lover, a photography enthusiast, or just looking for a relaxing escape, Gardens by the Bay is a must-visit.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
During my recent visit to Singapore, one of the most memorable places I explored was Gardens by the Bay. This remarkable attraction is a perfect example of how nature, technology, and sustainability can come together to create something truly extraordinary.

From the moment I entered the gardens, I was impressed by the sheer scale and beauty of the place. The landscape is meticulously maintained, featuring a stunning variety of plants, flowers, and themed gardens from around the world. The iconic Supertree Grove is undoubtedly the highlight, with its futuristic structures creating a breathtaking skyline, especially during the evening when they are beautifully illuminated.

I also had the opportunity to visit the conservatories, where the climate-controlled environments showcase unique plant species from different regions. Walking through these attractions felt like traveling across multiple ecosystems in a single day. The attention to detail, cleanliness, and visitor-friendly facilities were outstanding.

What impressed me most was Singapore’s commitment to environmental sustainability, which is reflected throughout the entire garden. Despite being located in the heart of a modern city, the gardens provide a peaceful escape where visitors can relax, take photographs, and appreciate nature.

Whether you are traveling with family, friends, or exploring solo, Gardens by the Bay is a must-visit destination. It offers a unique blend of natural beauty, innovation, and world-class design that leaves a lasting impression. My visit was truly unforgettable, and I would highly recommend it to anyone planning a trip to Singapore.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Gardens by the Bay is one of Singapore’s most impressive attractions and a place that keeps rewarding repeat visits. What started as a quick visit to the Supertrees has grown into an entire day of exploration, from the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest to the outdoor gardens, lakes, sculptures, and hidden corners scattered throughout the grounds

The Cloud Forest remains the highlight for me. Walking through the cool mist, towering ferns, orchids, and prehistoric-looking plants feels like stepping into another world. The elevated walkways offer fantastic perspectives, and the constantly changing displays make every visit feel fresh. The Flower Dome is equally beautiful, with seasonal floral exhibitions and meticulously maintained landscapes

Beyond the conservatories, there is so much to discover. The Supertrees are iconic, the outdoor gardens are peaceful and surprisingly diverse, and even simple walks around the lakes reveal interesting plants, wildlife, and photo opportunities. I particularly enjoyed wandering through areas that many visitors overlook, including the lily-covered ponds and the themed garden displays

Another pleasant surprise was discovering that the Conservatory Gift Shop leads into a complimentary Jurassic World Experience, which added an unexpected element of fun to the visit. Combined with convenient access via MRT, dining options such as Satay by the Bay, excellent facilities, and world-class landscaping, it is easy to spend an entire day here without getting bored

Old school taste. Dried noodle is tasty, very spicy but shiok. Remember to ask for less spicy if you are not so hot.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: 25 years never come this hawker centre. Heard  just renovation. Impressed with the food choices. So many good Chinese and Malay food. Wanton mee, kolo mee, yong tao fu, chicken rice, tutu kueh. Clean and well ventilated. Parking maybe hard but can park at kinetic mall and walk over.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: I discovered this new stall after the recent renovation of this very popular food centre. The steam fish extremely fresh and deliciously prepared. One of the best I have eaten which is so value-for-money for a healthier meal in food centre.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There are plenty of food options available, from malay to chinese and many others, making it a great spot to explore different flavors in one place. I also tried the famous putu piring, which was soft, a little bit sweet, and fragrant. The place can get quite busy but still worth to try.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Came here craving teh tarik, satay, and tu tu kueh — and all three wishes came true!

☕ Teh tarik was fragrant, smooth, and nicely pulled.
🍢 Satay was juicy, well-marinated, and smoky with delicious peanut sauce.
🍰 Tu tu kueh was soft, warm, and freshly made — comforting and nostalgic.

Perfect place if you want a mix of classic local flavours in one visit.
Will definitely come back again 👍

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
East Coast Lagoon Food Village is one of the best hawker centres in Singapore if you enjoy local food with a relaxed seaside atmosphere. The location is excellent, especially in the evening when the place becomes lively and you can enjoy the sea breeze while having dinner. There are many good food options here, from satay and BBQ seafood to local favourites, making it a great place for families, friends and visitors who want to experience Singapore hawker food.

The overall vibe is casual, energetic and very local. It can get busy during peak hours, but that also shows how popular this place is. A great spot for good food, local culture and a nice East Coast dining experience. Highly recommended.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
When we arrived here, it was dinnertime so getting a table for 5 took a little bit of time but not too long. Some of the food stalls have longer queues. Overall, the experience was great. Had some walk after dinner since the weather was good. There’s a skate park nearby and a bicycle rental that opens 24 hours.

Food here are great. Sugar cane juice is very sweet. Get the big one.

It’s right by the park so you can take a walk after food.

If you’re driving, parking was available even though the sign said full.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: East Coast Lagoon Food Village located at 1220 ECP, Singapore 468960 . It have been so long we didn't visit East Coast Lagoon Food Village and very disappointed with the food i ordered. We ordered from a halal certified stalls and Nasi goreng kampung rice is really not nice, and very over priced for 2 plate of rice $15 ....if is nice we are okay but unfortunately it taste horrible.  Luckily, we ordered satay from another stall and taste was average. Only the sugar cane drink slightly lifted up the overall meal. Will not be back to the same food stall.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Stall 38. My favourite bbq seafood stall.

Family run stall. Friendly and nice owners.

Price is worth it for the portion i ordered.

The crab was the highlight of the meal. It was so loaded with roe! Fantastic taste!

Highly recommend. 👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
There are hawker centres, and then there are places that feel like a full experience. This is one of them.

Set right by the sea, East Coast Lagoon Food Village has a kind of atmosphere that is hard to replicate elsewhere. The salty breeze, the smell of charcoal grills in the air, the hum of conversation and the sight of tables filled with shared plates. It feels lively, slightly chaotic, and very Singaporean.

What makes this place special is the sheer variety. You are genuinely spoilt for choice. Walk a few steps and you will see everything from BBQ Stingray, satay, and charcoal grilled chicken wings to classics like Hokkien Mee, oyster omelette, char kway teow and bak kut teh  ￼. It is one of those rare places where you can satisfy every craving at once.

Some stalls have built almost cult followings over the years. The famous Lagoon Carrot Cake is known for its wok hei and slightly caramelised edges, while places like Haron Satay 55 serve smoky skewers with rich peanut sauce. Seafood stalls offering sambal stingray and grilled squid are also a must, especially when eaten hot off the grill with that unmistakable smoky aroma  ￼.

The variety is what really stands out. You can go for a full seafood spread one night, then come back for something as simple as satay and sugarcane juice the next. It is the kind of place where sharing is almost essential, ordering a mix of dishes and letting the table slowly fill up.

It is not polished, and that is part of the charm. It is open air, sometimes busy, sometimes a bit smoky, but always full of energy.

A seaside hawker centre where the food is diverse, the atmosphere is lively, and the experience is as memorable as the meal itself. One of those places that captures the spirit of Singapore’s food culture in one sitting.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

Old Airport Road Food Centre exceeded my expectations with a great variety of Singapore heritage dishes. I also loved the creativity behind many stall names referencing places like Chong Pang, Kallang, and Blanco Court — adds local charm.

• Old Airport Road Fried Hokkien Mee (#01-99) – 4/5: Wet-style Hokkien Mee with decent wok hei, generous prawns, good value.

• Famous Old Airport Fried Oyster (#01-101) – 3/5: Soggy omelette, overcooked prawns, not worth $10.

• Chong Pang Huat BBQ (#01-90) – 3/5: Expensive, small portions, hygiene issues.

• Jin Fried Rice (#01-63) – 5/5: Fragrant mala fried rice with pork chop — excellent, must-try.

• Chuan Loo Bee Hokkien Food (#01-111) – 2/5: Tough pork ribs, odd sauce, disappointing.

• Whampoa Soya Bean (#01-68) – 4/5: Solid traditional desserts; soya drinks slightly diluted.

• Unkai Japanese Cuisine (#01-88) – 5/5: Generous salmon bowl, wallet-friendly, satisfying.

• Western Barbeque (#01-53) – 3/5: Chicken chop okay but lacks flavour; queues are long.

• Famous Kallang Airport Fried Oyster (#01-55) – 2/5: Oily, overwhelming, still hunting for a better version.

• Salt (#01-128) – 4/5: Aglio Olio with teriyaki chicken — decent but slightly pricey.

• Yi Ji Fried Prawn Noodles (#01-73) – 3.5/5: Carrot cake decent, noodles not outstanding.

• Xin Mei Xiang Lor Mee (#01-116) – 4/5: Double Fish Lor Mee decent; broth mediocre, some service issues.

• Heritage Tanglin Puff – 5/5: Singapore institution since 1952. Freshly fried puffs with generous fillings — curry potato and curry chicken are standouts. 6 for $12, great value.

• Ah Yee Hong Kong Roasted (#01-77) – 4/5: Three-meat combo rice ($10) — char siew well caramelised, roast pork had satisfying crackling, but roast duck was chewy and rice was dry. Chilli a nice touch. Would love a chicken option on the menu. Worth a visit for the char siew and roast pork.

Overall, a must-visit for variety and heritage flavours, though some stalls are hit-or-miss. Will keep exploring and updating!​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Old Airport Road Food Center is one of popular places for breakfast and lunch.  There are many nice food around such as Lor Mee, Hokkien Mee, Prawn Noddle, Chicken Rice, Roasted Pork and Duck, Western Food, Wanton Noddle.  There is also a new NTUC at level 2.  This food Center can be quite packed during peaked hours.  If you are around Kallang area, I will recommend you guys to drop by …

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Great hawker centre but it can get a little stuffy. Try to grab one of the round tables by the entrances or near the ventilation areas for more space and cool air.

Loved the lor mee! Char koay teow and prawn mee we’re decent too.

If you’re getting ais kacang or cendol, get it from the popular stall in the middle to avoid disappointment!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Very nice and patient uncle who runs this stall. I always thought sugar canes all tasted the same, until I tried this. Happened to find a seat in front of this stall and decided to just buy from him. The sugar cane was really fresh and had a clean taste (there was not that sugary aftertaste). Uncle was also telling me that his is not soaked in sugar syrup and yet it was still naturally sweet! Highly recommended!

I think $2 for a cup, extra for lemon.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: One of my favourite food centres. Great selection and food. Easy to get to via Bus 36 from Changi. Due for a nice power cleaning though.

The net playground is still there, which is a good thing and a unique attraction to this mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
If you are around Little India area, City Square Mall is actually a pretty nice place to chill and makan.
It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a very big mall with many renowned international brands like Levi’s, Breadtalk, and toy stores for the little ones like ToysRUs etc. Also sometimes there is a flea market inside the mall with some cool toys and books for a great price. Lots of restaurants as well in easy access.

Outside there is a musical park where you can play some soothing music or relax on the hammocks and there is also a eco bus, with environmental activities and it also spreads awareness about the environment. In all, it’s a great mall!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Kids friendly. Lots of activities for kids.  spot to visit with families and kids.. activities to do: cycling(rent family/child/adult), swimming, playing with sand, kids playground. Coffee bean,Mc D and Mr biryani food choices to choose from

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: This place is amazing for those who want to give kid a place to have fun. I took the 196bus got here and went underpass then to the playground. I think its a best place for kids. It’s even with toilet for kids and nearby got mcdonalds

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Wow. Best and biggest playground in Singapore maybe!  Great for kids. There are dedicated areas for toddlers as well as school age kids. But do keep me mind that big kids also go in the toddler section so need to watch out if you have a young toddler. rather crowded but still lots of space for everyone. Beach is just across so can play out in the sand too.  Washrooms with showers adjacent too. Bring water to keep hydrated and cool down, there is little breeze even though it is right next to ocean which I found surprising and was quite hot even though I visited in the late afternoon. McDonald's and other food places adjacent too for a quick snack.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Always a fun trip with family. Kids love Marine cove play area. With such a big playground, there's always something for everyone. The coffee shops and restaurants make it a complete deal however. Spend a few hours playing, walking or cycling in the area and come here to get some great food options. Coffee bean, briyani house, McDonald's and the East commune (new addition) take your pick 😄

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
My son joined the infant care group at Kinderland Marine Parade when we had just moved back to Singapore - he was just 9 months old. We credit the teachers and staff there with his smooth and happy transition to life in Singapore. Each of his class teachers shows interest and care for his wellbeing and communicates with us regularly about his progress or areas of concern.

It shows in the way he is excited to leave for school each morning - yelling the name of the front desk lady (one of his first words!) as he waves his shoes around to tell us that we need to leave now! Thank you!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Both my kids go to Kinderland at Marine Parade, and we've been pretty happy with it overall. The teachers are genuinely caring and patient — you can tell they're invested in the kids, not just going through the motions. My two have grown a lot in confidence and independence since starting there, and they actually enjoy going, which says a lot.

Overall, we’re happy with the experience with Kinderland MP

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: My sons enjoy going to Kinderland MP! The teachers are genuinely caring, nurturing and encouraging. The learning environment is great and I’ve seen so much growth and confidence in them since they’ve joined!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Kinderland Marine Parade has been my choice for my 2 kids since they were born. The teachers has always been putting in more than the extra mile needed, not only do they focus on academic it is the values that they incorporate to the children that is definitely worth mentioning. A special shout out to Fu laoshi, Ms Ying and Ms Gwen who has definitely frame up my child to be confident and independent. Thank you.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Highly recommended preschool! Principal is very caring and very interactive with the parents. Teachers are very engaging and willing to go above and beyond to help your child. CSO at the front desk is very welcoming and is always there when your child is in need of help! If you enrol your child at this centre, they’ll learn how to play the Yamaha Electone (as well as other instruments), learn how to perform confidently on stage, experience outdoor walks to East Coast Park and have the option of learning either Chinese OR Japanese.

Our eldest son has graduated from this preschool and we currently have two children attending the Marine Parade branch. Recently, we were involved in the Kinderland Sports Fiesta where the K1/K2 children participated in the ONLY Preschool Marching Band in Singapore. This is currently offered to only the Marine Parade and Yio Chu Kang branch. The Sports Fiesta involved parent-child sport activities as well as events like ‘Daddy’s Tug-of-War’ which was a highlight for all! It was a very good family bonding event where everyone came together. On top of the Sports Fiesta, the children have been involved in performing Lion Dances for Chinese New Year and performed a musical piece at a National Day event. End of year, they’ll be performing again on stage for their end of year concert, where they’ll be showcasing their musical talents. Bonus; your child will graduate learning how to play the Yamaha Electone as it is part of their curriculum!

Besides the Performing Arts, the children will have the opportunity to go on many excursions and have weekly outdoor walks around the neighbourhood (this includes East Coast Park and your local NTUC!). If you’d like to engage in more physical activities, the centre offers JACPA enrichment class in the afternoon. Not only do they offer JACPA but also Japanese language class as well!

Our children had no prior experience learning Chinese however they can now engage in Chinese conversation thanks to the dedicated teachers at Marine Parade. The centre also offers Japanese classes which has become very popular over the past few years. Some Japanese children graduate with the ability to speak English, Chinese AND Japanese as the main two form class teachers are English-speaking and Chinese-speaking.

Overall, highly recommended preschool as they offer so much for your child. Our eldest son was P1-ready and was able to transition with ease upon graduating from Marine Parade. Many of the students from this centre graduate and attend the neighbouring sought out Primary School. Thank you Kinderland for helping our children grow!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
NEX is a big, convenient mall that’s hard to beat for errands or a casual day out. It’s right by Serangoon MRT and the bus interchange, so getting in and out is easy. Inside, it’s bright, clean, and well-air-conditioned; even on weekends when it’s busy, the walkways are wide enough that you can still move comfortably.

Food options are plentiful, everything from quick bites to sit-down spots, and the food court is efficient with staff constantly wiping tables, so turnover is fast. Shopping is just as varied, with fashion, beauty, tech, and a large supermarket, which makes it great for one-stop “get everything done” runs. Families will appreciate the clear signage and clean restrooms, plus plenty of places to take a break if you’re with kids or older folks. Overall, a reliable place to eat, shop, and cool off, practical for a lunch stop and pleasant enough for an unhurried weekend stroll.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Huge shopping mall connected to Serangoon MRT. Has all outlets that you need for shopping and food.
Very accessible. Can get a bit crowded on weekends.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Probably the busiest, most bustling mall in the heartlands. It’s a decent size with a pretty good tenant mix, but word on the street is that the old Isetan spot is being turned into a bunch of boring tuition centres and a Kiztopia. If that’s true, we’ll probably be visiting a lot less.

There’s also been a massive proliferation of PRC restaurants in recent years, which makes us even less inclined to frequent the place. We might drop by when Chin Mee Chin finally opens, but we aren’t going to visit as much as before.

P.S The elevators are always under maintenance or not working.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Good Location Shopping!!!
But saw such behaviours of youngsters is totally embarrassing and flabergusted, giving others no place to walk via bulldozing in a row.
Saw them few times doing this in 2025 and 2 times in 2026. Video was captured when I changed my handphone and was trying out it's functions.
The worst is, this group have been doing this bulldozing walks everywhere they go, even Tourists has been complaining on TikTok regarding such behaviours when they visited Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The management of NEX mall (Serangoon MRT) must be fast asleep. A couple of their escalators (to L2 & B1, see photos) have been out of service for quite a few months and no repair work has been initiated. These escalators are covered with cladding and nobody has been spotted working on them! Rather than have them fixed, signs are instead posted to direct customers to other (working, for the time being) escalators. This smacks of apathy and a pathetic lack of professional pride & customer care. Whoever is at the top needs to deal with it - unless, of course, he/she/they also can’t be bothered. In which case, the problem is endemic.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: My P4 son has had the privilege to be taught English by Mr John Anthony, an exceptional teacher. From the very first lesson, our son shared that he never felt rushed nor discouraged when things were difficult, and that Mr Anthony always took the time to explain concepts clearly and at a comfortable pace. What truly set Mr Anthony apart was his ability to make every lesson genuinely fun and enjoyable; our son would come home excited to share what he had learned, which, as any parent knows, is no small feat. Beyond keeping lessons engaging, he had a remarkable talent for motivating our son to push past his comfort zone and strive to do better — not through pressure, but through encouragement and belief in his potential. The progress we have seen has been tremendous, and we have no doubt that it is a direct reflection of his dedication and passion for teaching. We are deeply grateful for everything he has done.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Teacher John Anthony is a very kind and helpful educator. He knows exactly how to guide children to make steady progress step by step. His classes are absolutely worth attending — his engaging and lively teaching style motivates students to learn in a joyful and positive environment. My daughter really likes him a lot!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Ms Siu Yan is one of the best teachers in TLL. My child achieved an AL1 in the PSLE Science exam thanks to the teacher’s guidance. ms Siu Yan is extremely patient and meticulous, and the teaching methods are engaging and effective. My child loves the Science lessons very much.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Hi, "graduated" student here. Would definitely recommend this tuition centre, especially Mr Ben Tan of the English Department in TLL Marine Parade. Helped me score AL1 for my PSLE English, and has his own English resources for different components of the PSLE papers, especially compo. He expects discipline and civicness in his classes. Definitely he can be strict, but he can also crack jokes and laugh with you. He is definitely fair towards all his students whenever we used to talk during compo writing and wouldn't scold you if you did nothing wrong. With all his resources, he ensures you walk into the exam hall prepared.
Other teachers I'd surely recommend are Ms Cheong and Mr Marcus Tan of the Maths Department. Mr Marcus makes lessons engaging and fun while ensuring he doesn't go off topic of the curriculum. I miss his candy machine so much! Ms Cheong is also a very caring teacher, and is always ready to help her students. During PSLE, she also created a Whatsapp groupchat for us students to post questions that we have difficulties doing in the Whatsapp group and she will help us. She also doesn't focus on the answer, but rather the logic behind the method used to tackle the question. She also always printed out extra practices for us to do. miss the mixue she bought us during PPP! (psle preperatory course)
For Science, Ms Vanessa Ong is a competent and patient teacher who expects the best out of her students for them to reach their full potential. I still remember her helping me and holding me back after class to teach me mcqs with my friends.
I created genuinely good memories and made lasting friendships from my time in TLL! I still keep some of the resources like notes and books TLL gave us (especially Mr Ben's notes ;) and i genuinely wouldn't mind doing the whole PSLE thing again. if my teachers are seeing this, tysm for all ur guidance in psle!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Love this Mountbatten MRT station location. Previously had to take a complicated bus route home from Kallang station but not anymore. It conveniently connects to town specifically Dhoby Ghaut MRT station. The staff are really professional and efficient too; there was once in recent years several of us commuters were rendering assistance to someone that lost balance and fell, and the staff were swift in activating all the right protocols too. Always in good hands at this station 🙏🏻

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
We travelled from Dhoby Ghaut MRT Station to Lorong Chuan MRT Station (Sat around 9pm). We were aware of the scheduled service disruption (due to some ongoing tunnelling works) and required to switch trains at Mountbatten and Paya Lebar Stations.
The crowd management measures at Mountbatten and Paya Lebar Stations were opined to be effective and organised. There were additional staffs and crowd marshals deployed to manage and direct crowds. There were also ample informative signages, barriers and dividers put up. Observed there were some ‘confused’ commuters but the staffs were there to offer assistance and direction and helped them navigate from one platform to another. The multi-age teams were very patient, alert and empathetic. They strived to ensure commuter safety and ensure smooth-flowing crowds (especially during boarding and disembarking from the trains and riding the escalators).

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: It's a beautiful place we can see Singapore and Malaysia at that same time. When I went it's full rainy and I took photoshoot there. We want to spend peace area I will suggest The Woodlands Waterfront. Beautiful silent area. We will hear only Air sound, Water sound.

review_text:

Ryan chou
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Woodlands Waterfront Park is one of my favorite spots to unwind. The park offers a beautiful, relaxing view of the Johor Strait, especially during sunset. The waterfront promenade is perfect for a peaceful stroll or a bike ride, and there’s plenty of open space for picnics with family and friends.

I also love the iconic jetty—it’s a great place to take in the sea breeze and watch the boats go by. The park is well-maintained, with clean paths and plenty of benches to sit and enjoy the scenery. Highly recommended for anyone looking to escape the city hustle without going too far!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Our family including our toddler has been waiting eagerly for this MRT Station on the Thomson-East Coast Park Line (TEL), and I’m so glad we got to be part of the Public Preview today before it officially goes into service on 23 June 2024! 🚝✨🇸🇬 The station is spacious and quite deep underground. Everything is so brand new. The trains ran smoothly despite the higher volume of passengers today. The toilets are fantastic. There are lifts in place for those that need it. Our 2-year-old had such a wonderful time tonight here at Marine Parade, thank you! 🙏🏻 This will bring about a whole new level of accessibility and convenience to this neighbourhood, and is well connected underground to many parts of Marine Parade including Parkway Parade.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
I had a very disappointing experience with an SMRT platform staff member. A toddler in our group was extremely hungry and distressed, yet we were told the child was not allowed to drink milk on the platform.

While rules are important, empathy and common sense matter—especially when it comes to young children. The staff member was rude in their approach and continued to glare at us on multiple occasions afterward, which felt uncomfortable and unnecessary. This made an already stressful situation worse for both the child and the parents.

I hope SMRT places more emphasis on compassion, discretion, and customer service when training frontline staff. A little empathy goes a long way in creating a more caring and humane public transport experience.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Its easy you can pay with your bank card ,and the map shows you the stops. Safe environment for everyone.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
The long wait is over! Incredibly happy that the Thomson East Coast Line (TEL) has finally arrived (exact date: 23 June 2024).

Its design is simple. Love the dark green colour scheme of the underground platform.

It has a big sheltered parking space for bicycles - cyclists rejoice!! Have not gone to all the new stations yet. I saw sheltered bicycle parking space at the Marine Terrace station too.

The station is situated just beside the Hawker Centre & McDonald’s, and is a few minutes walk to Parkway Parade.

Going to the beach from this station would take you around 10 min. Head towards the NTUC FairPrice at Marine Drive, and the underpass to the beach is right behind.

i12 is close by too - a few min walk if you exit from the right exit.

I’m beyond happy that the TEL is finally here in this part of our island 🫶🫶🫶

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
This MRT is a game changer for Parkway Parade and the hawkers food market. It's so convenient located that once you come out of the exit, you see PP and next to it the food market. At least 40 stalls here for you to choose and all the shop beside it, 2 coffee shops all withing 5 min walking distance. You got banks here, like OCBC, POSB, UOB, DBS.....
It's more bustling and crowded too. Good and Bad. Anyway, always look at the bright side of life.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
We visited Maxwell Food Centre at around 8:00 AM on a Monday, so it was still relatively quiet with very few queues. Unfortunately, the famous chicken rice stall was closed, which was a bit disappointing. This was actually my second visit, and I still haven't had the chance to try it!

Thankfully, there are plenty of other stalls serving chicken rice, so we simply picked one without a line and it didn't disappoint. In fact, that's the beauty of Maxwell Food Centre. There are so many food options to choose from, and everything we tried was delicious. The drinks were great too, and the prices are very affordable. Those in the pictures are only some of those we found really delicious.

Whether you're craving local favorites or just looking for a good meal, Maxwell Food Centre never disappoints. If you're in Singapore, make sure to stop by for delicious food at budget-friendly prices. Just keep in mind that if you're hoping to try the famous chicken rice, it's worth checking the stall's opening hours before you go!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There’s a wide variety of food and the laksa was absolutely amazing. It’s cheaper if you pay cash instead of card. The staffs in charge of cleaning the trays were really proactive. We didn’t try the super crowded stalls, but the ones we had were pretty solid in terms of quality. Also no bad odor

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I was here in the evening time and most of the stalls were closed for the day.
But I am very fortunate of finding a chicken rice shop still open.
Uncle Louis Famous chicken rice. Uncle was very hands on. He takes each customer's order attentively and gives suggestions along the way.
I ordered chicken with soup and veggies. Very tender, fresh veggie sides and hot rice. Yummy! Welcome local dinner on my 1st night in Singapore.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A step back in time with fantastic food variety at really bargain prices. Only one of the 3 stall rows has high velocity fans for extra cooling. A few obvious winners among the vendors. We had Vietnamese  Tofu and noodles and vegetarian rice paper rolls.
A local character has found a way to always get his favorite seat - Xtra load rap music. He managed to get a whole table under a fan for himself during the lunch peak. There's someone who understands marketing!
A fantastic authentic street food location that has a busy lunch peak but is much quieter otherwise.
The lady selling fresh crushed fruit and sugar can drinks is a standout nearthe China Town exit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: My favourite eating place in the city by far. So much to choose from, foodie heaven. There's always a good selection of shops open. Can be very busy at time with queues for the most popular places.  Don't be scared to share tables. When finished, put your tray in designated area before leaving.

review_text: I would like to express my sincere gratitude for the fantastic event held at the Chinese Swimming Club, Lattice Suite. A special shout-out goes to Edmund Lee and Andy Lam, whose outstanding assistance made the event a success. From the setup to accommodating special requests and providing excellent banquet support on the day itself, their hospitality was truly exceptional. The service was fast, efficient, and they went above and beyond to ensure everything ran smoothly. The food was delicious, with generous portions and a wide range of options to choose from. I highly recommend their services and look forward to working with them again!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Perfect hangout place for kids and adults to brunch there, tried their buffet there with the kids, there's something for everyone to eat! And not forgetting the swim there, big and small pool available, adults can also book the badminton court there for a game or two and bowling available. Everyone thoroughly enjoyed our half a day there at CSC 👍🏻

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
I am writing to formally raise a complaint regarding an incident involving a staff member, Zainul, at the club on the afternoon of 17 April.

At approximately 3:15 PM, I arrived at the club but had forgotten to bring my membership card. As such, my mother gave me her card to tap in, and we are under the same family account.

While attempting to tap in, Zainul approached me and forcibly wrestled the card out of my hand without any explanation. He then brought the card to the counter and kept insisting that I obtain a temporary card. I come here weekly and he recognised me as a frequent visitor; however, he was dismissive when I attempted to explain that I was a regular member who had simply forgotten my card on that particular day.

Later, he even escalated the situation further by informing the staff at Man Zhu Café to check my card as well. The situation left me feeling uncomfortable and singled out.

I fully understand and respect the club’s policies regarding access and identification. However, I believe the situation could and should have been handled more professionally, especially since I come here weekly. Forcibly trying to snatch a card out of my hands and getting fellow staff to single me out was so unprofessional and left me feeling unwelcome. This is extremely inappropriate for a club.

As someone who has visited CSC frequently over many years, this experience was deeply disappointing. I hope the management will take this feedback seriously and review the conduct expected of staff in handling such situations. I would appreciate reassurance that similar incidents will be addressed appropriately in the future.

review_text: There is a basketball court there which is in quite good condition. However, the badminton court is bad. Firstly, the floor is slippery and dirty. Basically impossible to play there. constant wind and slippery court. Waste of money sia. You come here you come for the cafe or to play basketball. Just dont play badminton here. Alsi in a very inconvenient location. Except that it is near a very small hawker centre. Not near Macpherson MRT, need to take bus from there to reach here. Overall a bad experience.
